metadata.title: Straight Into Canvas — AI+1 series 4/4
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: Italian short form (HAI budget) — HAI takes only the shortest forms (Hi · Ola · Hej · Ciao); never spell out HumanitariansAI in the greeting (the footer chip does that). Wagwan n/a (Bear only).
metadata.purpose: CUSTOMER pitch, not investor pitch: sell custom workshops, custom courses, and $1 Kindle books from a nonprofit doing good, and send viewers to the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat carries a short serif spark line. HANDOFF LAW: 2nd-to-last beat is the composer with a paste-ready action, greeting 'Your turn.'. OUTRO LAW: restate the title, poster serif, terracotta period. House grammar: >=1 PREDICT beat; verdict page carries the phase gate; decisions.json + DECISIONS.md ship beside this sheet.

beats.narration_text: … this is Liam, in for Bear. Here's the question on the table: can a custom AI course load straight into Canvas without an IT ticket, an integration project, or a vendor call? It can — and the mechanism is a file format Canvas already speaks natively.

props.command: can this load straight into Canvas?

beats.narration_text: The format is .imscc — Canvas's own import standard. You upload a zip, confirm the import, and your course is standing: modules in place, videos embedded, exams live. No integration project. No ticket queue. No vendor review to schedule before you can teach.

props.heading: The format is .imscc — Canvas's own import standard.
props.body: You upload a zip, confirm the import, and your course is standing: modules in place, videos embedded, exams live. No integration project. No ticket queue. No vendor review to schedule before you can teach.

beats.narration_text: If your institution wants humans in the room, Humanitarians AI can deliver the training directly. Your team, your actual cases — their instructors run the session. This is the difference between receiving a file and having someone walk your faculty through it.

props.heading: If your institution wants humans in the room, Humanitarians AI can deliver the t
props.body: Your team, your actual cases — their instructors run the session. This is the difference between receiving a file and having someone walk your faculty through it.

beats.narration_text: The white-label option flips the arrangement entirely. Humanitarians AI builds the workshop, your own subject-matter experts deliver it, and your organization's brand is on the front. Your bench gains the material. Your clients and learners see you.

props.heading: The white-label option flips the arrangement entirely.
props.body: Humanitarians AI builds the workshop, your own subject-matter experts deliver it, and your organization's brand is on the front. Your bench gains the material. Your clients and learners see you.

beats.narration_text: Here's a predict beat. Before the reveal, count up the total lock-in cost: platform fee, seat license, annual renewal. You're buying a course — how much recurring cost would you expect from a typical ed-tech vendor? Take a second and commit to a number.

props.heading: Here's a predict beat.
props.body: Before the reveal, count up the total lock-in cost: platform fee, seat license, annual renewal. You're buying a course — how much recurring cost would you expect from a typical ed-tech vendor? Take a second and commit to a number.

beats.narration_text: The answer is zero. The .imscc cartridge is a file you own. The zip is a folder you own. The transform rights travel with them — if Humanitarians AI disappeared tomorrow, your course keeps running exactly as it was. That's how course material should work: the buyer outlasts the seller.

props.heading: The answer is zero.
props.body: The .imscc cartridge is a file you own. The zip is a folder you own. The transform rights travel with them — if Humanitarians AI disappeared tomorrow, your course keeps running exactly as it was. That's how course material should work: the buyer outlasts the seller.

beats.narration_text: Let's put the whole delivery picture on one page. One: upload the .imscc cartridge directly to Canvas — modules, videos, and exams land in place with no integration work. Two: optional custom training from Humanitarians AI if you want facilitated onboarding. Three: white-label workshops your own experts deliver under your brand. And underneath all of it — files you own, no platform, no seats, no renewal.

artifactLines: .imscc export → direct Canvas upload: modules, videos, exams standing in place.
Custom training and live workshops, delivered by Humanitarians AI.
White-label: we build it, your experts deliver it, your brand carries it.
Hiring us funds a 501(c)(3): fellows, mentorship, and free AI education.
Phase gate: files you own · no platform, no seats, no renewal · your course outlives us.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — and the clock starts the day the zip arrives. The handoff prompt here will have Claude unpack the .imscc, list exactly what lands in Canvas, and draft your week-one announcement to students. Ten minutes from zip to announcement. Paste it.

props.command: When my AI+1 zip arrives: open the .imscc, list exactly what will land in Canvas — modules, videos, exams — and draft my week-one announcement to students.

beats.narration_text: Straight into Canvas — that's the delivery story for AI+1. The canon is a dollar, the custom version is a quote away, and every purchase funds the nonprofit mission. Liam, in for Bear.

props.title: Straight Into Canvas.
